title: Government softens stance on repossession of Delhi Gymkhana Club premises
content: The Centre clarified in Delhi High Court that June 5 was an "option" for the Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate, not a forced handover. This statement offered relief to club members and employees after a previous letter indicated a forceful repossession for defence purposes. The government's revised stance suggests a more restrained approach to the club's eviction.

title: US Immigration authority issues clarification for H-1B visa holders
content: USCIS has clarified a new Green Card policy. This policy could have required many to apply from abroad. However, USCIS now states that applicants with national interest or economic benefit may continue their current process. This offers relief to many H-1B visa holders and tech industry workers. The agency is reasserting its interpretation of immigration law.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: 'We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system'
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Chiefs linked to ex-Patriots WR amid Rashee Rice legal uncertainty
content: The former New England Patriots’ wide receiver, Stefon Diggs, has remained an unrestricted free agent since the team released him in March after a dominating performance. Amid this, the star player has now been linked to the Kansas City Chiefs for the upcoming season, considering the messy situation involving Rashee Rice as Rice continues to be in jail for the next month.

title: Amanda Nunes and Nina Nunes’ 2026 net worth and lifestyle
content: MMA power couple Amanda and Nina Nunes boast an estimated combined net worth of $5-7 million by 2026. Amanda's historic dual-division championship reign and lucrative UFC career, coupled with Nina's steady run and coaching, fueled their financial success.  Their investments and private lifestyle underscore a remarkable journey from combat sports to respected family life.

title: Fact check: Is Shai Gilgeous-Alexander sidelined for Game 5 vs Spurs?
content: Social media claims of Shai Gilgeous-Alexander being out for Game 5 with an oblique injury are false; he is healthy and expected to start. The rumor likely stems from an old abdominal strain. The real injury concerns for OKC are Jalen Williams (questionable, hamstring) and Ousmane Dieng (out, soleus), impacting their Game 5 prospects.

title: Over 230 passengers evacuated using slides at BLR after smoke on taxiing IndiGo aircraft
content: An IndiGo flight from Bengaluru to Chennai experienced a smoke scare while taxiing, prompting an emergency evacuation of passengers via slides. The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) is investigating the incident, which marks the second such evacuation for IndiGo this month. All passengers and crew were reported safe.

title: Petrol pain: How fuel price hike complicates RBI’s inflation fight
content: Every Re 1 hike in petrol, diesel, CNG, and LPG costs feeds into inflationary pressures in the economy, and also indirectly impacts discretionary spending, hence hitting growth channels.

title: Shahana Goswami recalls love story with Milind Soman despite age gap
content: Shahana Goswami has spoken candidly about her past relationship with actor-model Milind Soman, years before he married Ankita Konwar in 2018. In a recent interaction, Shahana recalled how she first connected with Milind as a teenage fan and reflected on why their relationship eventually came to an end.

title: 'Would like to forget': Shubman Gill admits GT's nightmare vs RCB
content: Gujarat Titans suffered a crushing defeat against Royal Challengers Bengaluru in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1, failing to chase a record 254/5. Skipper Shubman Gill admitted the team's fielding and bowling were below par, leading to a heavy loss. GT now heads to Qualifier 2 after RCB secured a direct final spot.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for May 27, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 27, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'I'm not worried about ... ': Rajat Patidar after RCB storm into IPL final
content: Rajat Patidar's explosive 93 off 33 balls, featuring 9 sixes, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a record 254/5 and a dominant 92-run victory over Gujarat Titans. This commanding performance secured RCB's spot in their second consecutive IPL final, showcasing a fearless, attacking mindset.

title: TN political crisis: Rebel AIADMK MLA backs EPS, submits apology letter
content: AIADMK faces internal turmoil as Hosur MLA P Balakrishna Reddy backs Edappadi K Palaniswami, signaling a potential truce. This follows multiple rebel MLAs submitting apology letters to avoid disqualification after voting against party lines. The party's strength in the Assembly has dwindled due to recent resignations, intensifying a 'horse-trading' row.

title: 'Don 3' fallout: Can a film union 'ban' Ranveer Singh from working?
content: The Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) has issued a non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh over his exit from Don 3.  Legal experts question the directive's binding nature, stating it's an internal measure, not legally enforceable. Ranveer's team argues FWICE lacks jurisdiction over this civil dispute, and he can challenge the ban in court.

title: Cuba Appeals For International Help As US Intensifies Pressure
content: Donald Trump has openly mused about taking over communist Cuba, saying that after the US attack in January that ousted Nicolas Maduro in Venezuela, Havana could be his next target for military action and big change.

title: North Korea Tests New Multi-Purpose Missile Launch System: Report
content: South Korea said on Tuesday the North fired several projectiles, including a ballistic missile, off its west coast, the latest in a flurry of tests this year. Seoul said the missiles flew about 80 kilometres (50 miles).

title: Case Filed Against Mamata Banerjee Over 2025 "Ganda Dharma" Remark
content: The complaint alleges that Mamata Banerjee's comments hurt the sentiments of followers of Sanatan Dharma and promoted communal disharmony.

title: Canada To Impose 21-Day Isolation For Travellers From Ebola-Hit Region
content: Canadian officials also said that starting Wednesday, they are pausing final decisions on immigration applications for people from affected countries for 90 days.

title: Israel Says Targeted New Hamas Armed Wing Chief In Gaza Strike
content: Gaza's civil defence agency, which operates as a rescue service under Hamas, meanwhile said at least three people were killed and 20 wounded in an Israeli airstrike in the Rimal neighbourhood in western Gaza City.

title: 1 Dead, Several Injured After Chemical Tank Implodes At Washington Mill: Report
content: A chemical tank rupture at the Nippon Dynawave Packaging facility in Longview, Washington resulted in multiple critical injuries and at least one fatality, ABC News reported on Tuesday, citing authorities.

title: Pregnant Woman Jumps To Death In Delhi; Family Alleges Dowry Torture: Cops
content: The victim's family alleged, within a month of the marriage, the husband and his family started demanding gold and cash and subjected her to mental harassment.

title: Behind Red Fort Blast: Fake IDs, Online Shopping Spree, Homegrown Bomb Lab
content: The high-intensity vehicle-borne IED blast that rocked the national capital on November 10 last year left at least 11 people dead and injured several others.

title: Delhi Cracks Down On Illegal Animal Sacrifice Ahead Of Bakrid
content: Ahead of Bakrid, the Delhi government has deployed Rapid Response Teams across all 13 districts to crack down on illegal animal sacrifice, unauthorised livestock trade and violations of festival guidelines in the capital.

title: Trump Reports 'Perfect' Annual Medical Checkup Ahead Of 80th Birthday
content: It was Trump's third medical examination since returning to office last year and follows mounting speculation about issues including bruising on his hands and apparent sleepiness during meetings.

title: Identify, Detain, Deport: Centre Forms Panel To Address Demographic Change
content: The committee will not only undertake a comprehensive assessment of the demographic changes across India due to "illegal migration and other anomalous factors" and analyse the situation but also suggest ways of addressing the situation including thro

title: "Look Beyond Karnataka": Inside Track Of Rahul Gandhi-Siddaramaiah Meeting
content: At the 35-minute meeting, Rahul Gandhi reportedly told Siddaramaiah that any decision must now be made with the 2028 assembly election and the 2029 Lok Sabha election in mind.

title: Delhi CNG Prices Have Jumped Rs 6 In 11 Days. Here's Why
content: With the latest hike, CNG prices in Delhi have risen from Rs 77.09 per kg before May 15 to Rs 83.09 per kg on May 26.

title: Pak May Need New Passports If It Accepts Trump's Abraham Accords Proposal
content: Pakistan said it was "under no compulsion" to accept such demands and reiterated that recognition of Israel remains tied to the creation of a sovereign Palestinian state.

title: Exclusive: Israeli Defence Official On Hamas Threat, Civilian Deaths In Gaza
content: In an exclusive interview with NDTV, Ben Cohen said Israeli forces are currently dealing with threats from Gaza, Lebanon and Iran-backed groups operating across the region.

title: 'Not Rarest Of Rare Case': Delhi Court Denies Death Penalty To Child Rape Convict
content: A Delhi court refused to award the death penalty to a man convicted of raping a four-year-old girl, saying the incident did not fall under the "rarest of rare" category.

title: 6 Months Into Marriage, Lucknow Woman Found Dead After Dowry Harassment
content: Her family has alleged that she faced repeated demands for dowry, including pressure for a four-wheeler, and that her in-laws murdered her before staging the scene to look like suicide.

title: Gujarat Sends Vets, Multiple Teams To Gir Amid Sudden Lion Deaths
content: Wildlife activists explain that the disease primarily spreads via "ticks" hosted by domestic cattle like cows and buffaloes, which migrate to the pride when lions scavenge on dead livestock or venture near forest borders.

title: Himachal panchayat polls: 78.53% polling in first phase, women voters outnumber men
content: Shimla recorded 79.80% voter turnout, Mandi 79.39%, Una 78.76%, Bilaspur 77.39%, Kangra 75.75%, Hamirpur 73.05% and Kinnaur 73.59%

title: Mundamveli residents lay day-long siege to GCDA headquarters
content: Residents raise fresh concerns over the alleged violation of mandatory distance norms separating the 110 kV EHT line passing through their neighbourhood from the twin towers.

title: Rajya Sabha defections, constitutional questions
content: The recent episode involving the Aam Aadmi Party and the invocation of the provisions of the 10th Schedule highlights the need for judicial clarity

title: Supreme Court to pronounce verdict on Bihar SIR tomorrow
content: Petitions had accused ECI of arbitrarily assuming powers to “determine citizenship” while overriding limitations clearly prescribed in parliamentary laws, rules and its own manual without providing ‘any good reason’

title: Suspected Ebola case triggers health alert in Bengaluru
content: Bengaluru District Surgeon and Medical Superintendent of the hospital, Anil Kumar Banagar, said the woman, suspected to have developed symptoms, had recently travelled from Uganda to Ahmedabad and had later arrived in Bengaluru

title: Andhra Pradesh emerges as a pioneer in AI-driven healthcare innovations, says Minister
content: Satya Kumar Yadav promotes AI-driven diagnostic tests, says that these not only provide accurate results but also improve the speed of delivery of healthcare services and make tests affordable for people

title: CBSE refutes claim that its marking portal was compromised
content: The Board’s statement comes as an ethical hacker claims he had exposed the vulnerabilities of the portal and complained to CERT-In in February

title: Fuel shortage hits pumps amid panic buying; OMCs call for calm, say supply is steady
content: Petrol pump owners report dwindling supplies and long queues as demand peaks due to farming activity; some impose curbs on sale fearing supply disruption; OMCs assure public of “normal operations with steady fuel availability”, advise against hoarding

title: Fast-track court frames charges against seven accused in Zubeen Garg death case
content: Four accused of murder based on SIT chargesheet; Garg died while swimming in Singapore in 2025; Assam CM claims he was murdered as part of a conspiracy; Singapore coroner ruled it an accidental drowning

title: Twisha Sharma death: Madhya Pradesh DGP defends Bhopal Police as CBI takes over case, launches probe
content: Kailash Makwana’s statement comes amid several allegations made by Twisha Sharma’s family such as institutional bias and procedural lapses by local police

title: India-U.S. and Quad frameworks on critical minerals take shape amid Chinese curbs
content: Agreement built upon continued engagements between two countries, including India becoming a signatory to the U.S.-led Pax Silica initiative on February 20, 2026.

title: Extreme heatwave hits A.P. as Vijayawada north soars to 47.3°C
content: The city felt like a furnace at 7 p.m. as well as the temperatures stood at 40°C, according to real-time weather information available on the Directorate of Economics and Statistics

title: Delhi High Court notice to Swati Chaturvedi on OpIndia’s plea against trial court order
content: The court said the material filed by OpIndia ‘prima facie nowhere shows’ that Chaturvedi was involved in ‘running extortion rackets’ as alleged

title: Andhra Pradesh launches initiative to clear water hyacinth and revive water bodies
content: MA&UD Principal Secretary says the unchecked growth of the weed has obstructed irrigation channels, degraded water quality, and disrupted aquatic ecosystems, posing challenges to agriculture and public health

title: Six killed and 21 injured as bus overturns on Lucknow-Agra Expressway
content: Eyewitness accounts indicate that the bus was traveling at a high speed when it collided with the divider and overturned

title: Quad FMs announce maritime, energy initiatives amidst Hormuz chokehold, tense South China Sea
content: FMs pledge collaboration on energy security, maritime surveillance, domain awareness; Quad joint statement calls for freedom of navigation, adherence to international law in Hormuz, Red Sea, South China Sea

title: Supreme Court to examine if law is diluting India’s wetland count
content: The Convention expressly included both natural and artificial wetlands, whether permanent or temporary, without distinction, senior advocate Gopal Sankaranarayanan and advocate Anindita Mitra submitted for the petitioners

title: CNG prices hiked for fourth time in eleven days in Delhi, North India; latest by ₹2/kg
content: Since the first hike on May 15, CNG prices in Delhi and across several cities of Uttar Pradesh, Haryana and Rajasthan, have cumulatively increased by ₹6 per kg

title: One dead, two hurt in Assam police encounters
content: A man accused in an April 24 armed robbery in Nagaon district was killed, while two ULFA-linked extortion operators in Sivasagar district received gunshot wounds

title: Traders oppose move to install Nehru statue at APMC Yard in Kalaburagi
content: The Gunj Market area witnesses a bandh as traders shut down business establishments and suspend commercial activities in protest

title: SC asks Uttar Pradesh, panel to frame development plan for Vrindavan's Banke Bihari temple
content: A Bench headed by Chief Justice of India says the State should “come forward” for the development of “secular infrastructure facilities” in and around the temple complex

title: Kerala Waqf Board says it was ‘forced’ to upload Munambam data after Farook College’s stand
content: However, those in college managing committee has defended the decision not to post the data, stating that Munambam was not a Waqf property and therefore the information was not uploaded on UMEED portal

title: No clarity on next Quad leaders summit, sparking questions about the group’s future
content: As officials struggle to pin down the schedules of Mr. Trump, Mr. Modi, Ms. Takaichi, and Mr. Albanese, there is speculation that the Quad will be downgraded to an FM-level meet or occur on the sidelines of other gatherings

title: Watch: India, U.S. strike Critical Minerals deal amid China concerns | Above the Fold | 26.05.2026
content: From the QUAD meeting in New Delhi and India-U.S. critical minerals deal to fresh AIADMK defections to the TVK and rising CNG prices - here are the top stories of the day.

title: Poultry farmers advised heatwave strategies amid rising chicken mortality
content: Farmers are advised to supply fresh water and feed to chicks, sprinkle water to decrease heat and follow scientific methods to dispose the dead birds to prevent diseases, says Animal Husbandry officials

title: Kerala Chief Minister misleading people in Munambam issue, alleges CPI(M)
content: Chief Minister, who had earlier offered to resolve the issue in 10 minutes, should explore the possibilities for solving it rather than complicating it. The government should attempt to find a lasting solution to the issue through legal means, says CPI(M) Ernakulam district secretariat

title: SC stays HC order allowing truants to take exams
content: the HC ordered no student to take exams on the grounds of lack of minimum attendance . the order could be considered a free pass by students to not attend classes . a bench of justices said all National Law Universities were suffering .

title: US state secretary Rubio calls Quad a linchpin but suspense still hangs over summit
content: a joint statement later said that they looked forward to the next summit but didn’t specify where it would be held . this was quite a contrast from the previous foreign ministers’ joint statement in July 2025 that said the next meeting would advance peace, stability, and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific .

title: Amid UK's record-breaking Celsius swelter, heat on Tories to reverse AC ban
content: the record-breaking may heatwave has raised political temperatures too . the conservatives pledge to reverse net-zero building regulations banning air-conditioning in new homes . tories say they will overturn Jenrick's regulations politely . he has defected to reform UK .

title: 1-time gene therapy can cut bad cholesterol by up to 62%: Study
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More

title: India rejects references to J&K in China-Pak statement
content:
India categorically rejected unwarranted references to the UT of Jammu & Kashmir in a joint statement between China and Pakistan that was issued after Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif's visit to China, saying the UTs of J&K and Ladakh will always remain integral parts of India. "No other country has the locus standi to comment on the same," said MEA's Randhir Jaiswal. "As regards the so-called CPEC projects, some of which are in India's territory, we resolutely reject any moves by other countries to reinforce or legitimise Pakistan's illegal and forcible occupation of these territories, impinging on India's sovereignty and territorial integrity," he said. On references to the so-called 'trans-boundary water resources cooperation' between China and Pakistan, the official said as the two countries, such a question does not arise.

End of Article

title: Gunman kills Gujarat woman working at store in US
content: masked assailant shot dead 47-year-old meghna patel during suspected robbery attempt . she had been living in the us for 10 years with her husband and two children . the cold-blooded killing has sent shockwaves in the Gujarati community .

title: CBI arrests student who got NEET questions from Pune headmistress
content: the total number of people arrested in the case has risen to 12 . the student allegedly received physics questions from school headmistress manisha Havaldar . he allegedly shared the questions on paper, photographed them and forwarded them to the student’s husband .

title: 'Avoid rabble rousing': Ladakh L-G tellls Wangchuk after 'support' to Cockroach Janta Party
content: ladakh l-G Vinai Kumar Saxena asked Sonam Wangchuk to desist from weaving a misleading and provocative narrative . he said he had a candid conversation with his wife, who championed her husband's cause when he was in detention . the activist says he is unsure of the origins of the party .

title: Gulmarg’s gondola shuts for a week over repairs, French firm audit
content: more than 300 tourists were trapped for up to seven hours . some were left dangling nearly 500 feet (150 metres) above the ground . gondola will remain suspended for a week to install a new gearbox .

title: Zubeen death case: Court frames murder charges against 4
content: the trial is scheduled to begin on June 8 . all seven accused, currently in judicial custody, will remain so until further orders . the fast-track court set up in connection with singer's death framed charges on Tuesday, including murder charges against four .

title: Hundreds try to flee to Bangladesh as Bengal CM Adhikari says quit India
content: dozens of Bangladeshis without the documents required to stay in India made a bee-line for the border exit in Bengal's North 24 Parganas on Tuesday . CM Suvendu Adhikari told all suspected infiltrators to leave or face action . the new BJP govt's "detect-delete-deport" strategy for illegal immigrants was implemented .

title: SC bins plea for urgent hearing to implement ban on cow slaughter
content: UP and Bihar were the first states to ban cow slaughter in 1955 . as many as 20 states and UTs have banned slaughter of cows . u.s. govt reiterated complete ban on cow slaughter without certification under 1950 Animal Slaughter Control Act .

title: Manipur scientist behind cosmic discovery laments state violence
content: Prabin Kalita is a journalist at The Times of India and is currently the Chief of Bureau (northeast). He has been reporting in mainstream Indian national media since 2001. He has been a field journalist reporting gamut of issues from India’s northeastern region and major developments in neighbouring countries like Myanmar, China, Bhutan and Bangladesh concerning India and northeastern region. He has been covering insurgency—internal and cross-border, politics, natural calamities, environment etc. He is a post-graduate in Geological Sciences from Gauhati University. Read More

title: House owner, 5 labourers die in septic tank in Odisha district
content: six people, including the owner of a house and his son, allegedly died of asphyxiation while trying to remove the centring of an under-construction septic tank . five labourers were hired for the job, with only one of them coming out alive, but unconscious . villagers called the fire services, which broke open the tank's cover and roof, recovering six bodies .

title: N Chandrasekaran unveils 3-year plan to cut losses in Tata new businesses
content: Reeba Zachariah is assistant corporate editor at The Times of India, Mumbai. She has been covering large Indian business houses such as the Tata Group. She also reports on a host of sectors like hospitality, retail, travel, liquor and consumer durables. She has been writing on mergers and  acquisitions and private equity. title: Being mute spectators no ground to prosecute in-laws for cruelty: SC
content: the in-laws of a woman cannot be prosecuted for cruelty or making dowry demands . such conduct is questionable on moral grounds, but does not automatically amount to criminal culpability . a bench of justices said that criminal law cannot be set in motion against every family member of the husband .

title: ‘Maachh-bhaat’ to be served twice a week at Maa canteens at Rs 5 in Bengal
content: all 367 maa canteens across the state will serve fish twice a week . the meal will be available for just Rs 5 . a new menu is expected to be introduced soon, with the specific days for serving fish likely to be finalised .

title: Delhi in talks with Moscow to buy additional batch of S-400 systems: Russian media
content: Russia and India are negotiating the delivery of an additional batch of S-400 triumf air defence systems . india is expressing interest in expanding its inventory beyond the five regimental sets ordered under the 2018 deal . the talks come as Moscow works to wrap up a $5.4 billion (approx Rs 40,000 crore) contract signed in 2018 .

title: Trump, nearing 80, continues war on aging, sleep, and other enemies
content: american president Donald Trump turns 80 next month . he has spent years presenting himself as less an aging president than a human monument to stamina, fast food, and pure willpower . now, amid a war on Iran, global economic stress, and growing scrutiny about his age, he headed out to a presidential repair shop .

title: Govt panel to examine 'unnatural demographic changes' due to illegal immigration
content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More

title: Dera chief Ram Rahim walks out on 30-day parole, his 16th since 2020
content: govt granted another 30 days' parole to Gurmeet Ram Rahim Singh . he is serving 20 years in prison for raping two women disciples . his release was his 16th since 2020 and his second this year .

title: 3 years on, acid attack survivor wins right to disability review
content: acid attack survivor, 28-year-old Reshma Qureshi, struggled for three years . she was assessed as "low vision" by a medical board last week . her disability ID card has now correctly certified her quantum of disability as 63% .

title: Sebi to reform corp bonds to reduce over-reliance on banks
content: regulator is looking at easing disclosure norms for debt-only issues . he said there was a need to develop bond market to reduce 'over-reliance' on banks . bond tokenisation is the process of converting a traditional bond into a digital token .

title: Quad unveils new global initiatives after year of uncertainty: Key takeaways
content: the quad announced a series of initiatives in key areas like critical minerals, energy security and maritime surveillance in the Indian Ocean . it also announced plans to build a port in fiiji, hoping to supercharge the grouping’s “global good” agenda . the meeting condemned the attacks on commercial shipping vessels and opposed future measures inconsistent with UNCLOS .

content: Manu Aiyappa has been with The Times of India for over two decades, currently serving as the Political Editor.
